chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: 7 Years After
entities_post_resolution.definition: A data retention policy specifying that customer/purchase records are retained for seven years after the customer's last purchase.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

entities_post_resolution.name: Category Name
entities_post_resolution.definition: Unique identifier for a category in the category dimension (TB_CATEGORY).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Unique category identifier | NO |
| CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO |
 | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO |
 | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree.

**Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | **Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique SKU code
- Human-readable product name
- Unit price in base currency (EUR)
- Active/inactive status
- Belongs to exactly one Category | Category         → TB_CATEGORY | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale.

**Primary Key**: PRODUCT_I

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A physical or mailing address tied to a customer that serves as the shipment destination.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | # Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID
- Valid email address (required for login)
- One or more delivery/billing addresses
- Geographic region for tax calculation
- Active/inactive status | **Business Rules**:
- Must place at least one order to remain active | **Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type | **Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | Customer Relationships:
- A Customer places zero or more Sales Orders | A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | Refund: A reimbursement issued

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A line item is a single product entry within a sales order, detailing the product reference, quantity, and the unit price at the time of purchase.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique Order ID
- References exactly one Customer
- Order date when transaction was initiated
- Total monetary value
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | **Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Synonyms**: line item, order detail, order row | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO |
| CUST_ID | INT | Customer who placed order | NO |
| ORDER_DATE | DATE | Date order was placed | NO |
 | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|-

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Payment is a transaction record that settles an amount against a single Sales Order, identified by a unique PAYMENT_ID and linked to the corresponding order (ORDER_ID); a Sales Order may have one or more Payments.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment          → PAYMENT | **Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A catalog item offered for sale on the platform, identifiable by a SKU and price, and belonging to exactly one category.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Definition**: A tangible or digital item offered for sale on the platform. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique SKU code
- Human-readable product name
- Unit price in base currency (EUR)
- Active/inactive status
- Belongs to exactly one Category | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | Product          → TB_PRODUCT | Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order Hdr
entities_post_resolution.definition: Represents the header record for each customer sales order, stored in the SALES_ORDER_HDR table and identified by the ORDER_ID primary key (containing order-level metadata such as CUST_ID and ORDER_DATE).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
SALES_ORDER_HDR
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Header record for each customer sales order.

**Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment)
 | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO |
| CUST_ID | INT | Customer who placed order | NO |
| ORDER_DATE | DATE | Date order was placed | NO |
 | **Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)
 | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID)

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipment is the logistics entity representing the physical delivery of products from a warehouse to a customer's address, identified by a unique Shipment ID and linked to a single sales order, with attributes such as source warehouse, destination address, shipped timestamp, tracking number, and status.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
SHIPMENT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: The physical delivery of Products from a Warehouse to a Customer's delivery address.

Business Context: Shipments represent the logistics side of order fulfillment. They track what items were sent, when, and to whom.

Key Attributes:
- Unique Shipment ID
- References exactly one Sales Order
- Source Warehouse
- Destination Customer Address
- Shipped timestamp
- Tracking number
- Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED | A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| WAREHOUSE_CODE | VARCHAR(20) | Source warehouse | YES | | **Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|--------

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A sales order is a formal transaction document that records a customer's purchase request for products, including an order ID, customer reference, date, line items, total amount, and status.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Definition**: A formal transaction document recording the agreement between the platform and a Customer to supply Products. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Order ID
- References exactly one Customer
- Order date when transaction was initiated
- Total monetary value
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | **Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates | SHIPMENT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: The physical delivery of Products from a Warehouse to a Customer's delivery address.

Business Context: Shipments represent the logistics side of order fulfillment. They track what items were sent, when, and to whom.

Key Attributes:
- Unique Shipment ID
- References exactly one Sales Order
- Source Warehouse
- Destination Customer Address
- Shipped t

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Price At
entities_post_resolution.definition: The per-unit price recorded for a product on a line item at the moment the order is placed, representing the historical price that remains fixed for that order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates | **Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

entities_post_resolution.name: Exactly One Customer
entities_post_resolution.definition: An integrity constraint indicating that an Order must reference exactly one Customer.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique Order ID
- References exactly one Customer
- Order date when transaction was initiated
- Total monetary value
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

entities_post_resolution.name: Bigint
entities_post_resolution.definition: A 64-bit integer data type used for primary key columns in a relational database, as exemplified by ORDER_ID being BIGINT and auto-increment.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment)
 | **Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | **Primary Key**: PRODUCT_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | **Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | **Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

entities_post_resolution.name: Formal Transaction Document
entities_post_resolution.definition: **Definition**: A formal transaction document recording the agreement between the platform and a Customer to supply Products.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Definition**: A formal transaction document recording the agreement between the platform and a Customer to supply Products.

entities_post_resolution.name: Future Price Updates
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates

entities_post_resolution.name: Multiple Addresses But
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Detail
entities_post_resolution.definition: **Synonyms**: line item, order detail, order row
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Synonyms**: line item, order detail, order row

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Row
entities_post_resolution.definition: **Synonyms**: line item, order detail, order row
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Synonyms**: line item, order detail, order row

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Of Units
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

entities_post_resolution.name: Status
entities_post_resolution.definition:
SHIPMENT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: The physical delivery of Products from a Warehouse to a Customer's delivery address.

Business Context: Shipments represent the logistics side of order fulfillment. They track what items were sent, when, and to whom.

Key Attributes:
- Unique Shipment ID
- References exactly one Sales Order
- Source Warehouse
- Destination Customer Address
- Shipped timestamp
- Tracking number
- Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core customer profile data including identifiers, contact details, regional segmentation, account creation timestamp, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, SKU, descriptive name, associated category, price, and whether the product is active in the catalog.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: Stores category information for products, including the category name and the hierarchical link to a parent category.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores the header information for customer sales orders, including identifiers, order timing, financial totals, status, and timestamps for payment confirmation, delivery, and creation.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores line-level details for each order, including which product was ordered, the quantity, unit price, and the calculated line amount.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores payment transactions linked to customer orders, including method, amount, status, and timestamps for confirmation and creation.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments, linking each shipment to its related order, the warehouse fulfilling it, shipment timestamp, tracking reference, and current shipment status.
